picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on WSH by 12.3pp — 59.2% fair against a 47.1% price. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.8pp), lineup quality (~+0.8pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is starter rest (~-0.6pp): The opposing starter is the better-rested side. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +113 still sits inside the playable range (to -123). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

picks.narrative: The model prices KC at 38.3% to win where the no-vig market implies 30.2%, a 8.8pp disagreement. Several smaller signals stack the same way — defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp), lineup quality (~+0.5pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-1.4pp): Beck Way 4.22 xFIP vs Tarik Skubal 2.62 xFIP. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +239 still sits inside the playable range (to +191). As a cluster edge rather than one dominant mismatch, it is a thinner, more fragile signal — worth sizing accordingly.

picks.narrative: IBP makes ATL 53.3% fair versus a 45.2% no-vig market price — a 8.2pp gap. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +122 still sits inside the playable range (to +103). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

picks.narrative: The model sees PIT as a 56.7% side; the market is pricing 49.5%, leaving a 6.7pp edge. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.5pp): Jared Jones 2.59 xFIP vs Matthew Boyd 4.21 xFIP. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): The model's defensive-efficiency adjustment leans to the opponent. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +100 still sits inside the playable range (to -111). The win condition is five-plus quality innings from Jared Jones and avoiding early bullpen exposure.

picks.narrative: A 4.8pp gap: IBP has LAD at 58.5%, the market at 53.1%. The largest lift is defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp): The model's defensive-efficiency adjustment favors the pick. The main pushback is home field (~-0.6pp): MET's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -116 still sits inside the playable range (to -120). This is value in the number more than the matchup — the model just makes LAD a better side than the price does.
